title: Proposed Protocol for Agrobacterium-mediated Transformation of Tomato Cotyledons for HSP101
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5t91n","text":"This protocol describes the transformation of tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) cotyledons using Agrobacterium tumefaciens strain LBA4404 carrying the binary vector pPBV[Exp]-Bar-(CaMV 35S)\u003eath_HSP101. The method allows stable integration of the ath_HSP101 gene under the CaMV 35S promoter ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":54,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":93,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b6b6d","text":"This protocol is intended as a proposed educational and research-planning workflow for future laboratory validation. It has not yet been experimentally validated by the author. All experimental implementation should be conducted only in an approved laboratory under supervision of qualified personnel and in accordance with institutional biosafety procedures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5bu3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"es40m","text":"All plant tissue culture work should be performed using aseptic technique to reduce contamination. Media, instruments, culture vessels, and work surfaces should be sterilized before use. Explants should be handled gently to avoid excessive tissue damage, as this may reduce regeneration efficiency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2blml","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7q3","text":"The transformation efficiency and regeneration response may vary depending on tomato cultivar, seedling age, explant condition, bacterial culture condition, antibiotic concentration, and plant growth regulator composition. Therefore, optimization may be required before successful transformation and regeneration can be achieved.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fkm4j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dc3an","text":"Putative transformed plants should not be considered confirmed transgenic plants until molecular confirmation has been completed. Suggested confirmation methods may include PCR-based detection of the inserted gene and/or additional molecular analysis where available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5q3v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ambds","text":"All genetically modified plant material, bacterial cultures, and contaminated consumables should be handled, contained, and disposed of according to institutional biosafety and GMO waste-management requirements.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9r5uo","text":"Prepare all culture media and sterilize materials before beginning the experiment. Work under sterile conditions inside a laminar flow hood when handling plant tissues.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ei4ba","text":"Ensure the following materials are ready:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dbhek","text":"Tomato seedlings (7–10 days old)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fo1ed","text":"Agrobacterium tumefaciens LBA4404 carrying the HSP101 vector","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lte6","text":"LB media and antibiotics","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6acom","text":"Murashige and Skoog (MS) plant culture media","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fig0","text":"Plant growth regulators","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ehs6a","text":"This proposed protocol involves Agrobacterium tumefaciens, antibiotics, and genetically modified plant material. steps: LB broth (1 L)
Dissolve: 10 g tryptone 5 g yeast extract 10 g NaCl Adjust pH to 7.0 . Autoclave at 121 °C for 15–20 min .
Kanamycin stock solution
Dissolve 500 mg kanamycin in 10 mL sterile water . Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter . Store at −20 °C. Use 50 µg/mL final concentration in LB media.
Preparation of Agrobacterium culture

Streak Agrobacterium tumefaciens LBA4404 on LB agar + kanamycin (50 µg/mL) . Incubate plates at 28 °C for 48–72 h . Select a single colony and inoculate into 5–10 mL LB broth + kanamycin . Incubate at 28 °C, 200–250 rpm for 16–24 h . Transfer 1.5 mL of culture into 50 mL LB broth + kanamycin and incubate again until turbid.
Preparation of transformation suspension
Centrifuge bacterial culture at 4000–5000 rpm for 10 min . Discard the supernatant. Resuspend the pellet in: 5% sucrose solution 0.05% Silwet-L77
Preparation of tomato cotyledon explants
Germinate tomato seeds under sterile conditions. After 7–10 days , excise cotyledons from seedlings. Cut cotyledons into small sections to expose wounded tissue suitable for infection.
Infection of explants
Immerse cotyledon explants in the Agrobacterium suspension. Incubate for 10–15 minutes with gentle agitation. Remove explants and blot dry on sterile filter paper.
Co-cultivation
Transfer infected explants to co-cultivation medium containing: MS medium 100 µM acetosyringone Incubate explants at 25 °C in darkness for 2–3 days .
Selection and shoot regeneration

Transfer explants to selection medium containing: MS medium 50 mg/L kanamycin 250 mg/L cefotaxime 3% sucrose 0.8% agar Add plant growth regulators: 1 mg/L BAP 0.1 mg/L NAA Incubate under 16 h light / 8 h dark photoperiod at 25 °C . (Shoots should emerge after 3–4 weeks .)
Root induction
Transfer regenerated shoots to rooting medium containing: ½ MS medium 0.5 mg/L IAA 3% sucrose Roots typically develop within 1–2 weeks .
Acclimatization
Transfer rooted plants to soil pots. Maintain plants in a greenhouse with controlled humidity. Gradually acclimate plants to normal environmental conditions.
